mission.title: longhorn: [IMPROVEMENT] Remove or Change Helm pre-upgrade hook to support ArgoCD
mission.description: [IMPROVEMENT] Remove or Change Helm pre-upgrade hook to support ArgoCD. This issue affects 16+ users.

steps.title: Identify longhorn upgrade symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your longhorn deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn -n longhorn --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review longhorn configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant longhorn configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
kubectl get configmap -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=longhorn
```
As of now, longhorn stopped working with ArgoCD, due to ArgoCD implementing helms post-upgrade hooks as `PreSync` (and they have no plans to change this, since they believe Helm charts should not use any pre/post upgrade hooks), causing it to run

steps.title: Apply the fix for [IMPROVEMENT] Remove or Change Helm pre-upgrade hook to…
steps.description:
The pre-upgrade hook is only for the helm to prevent any invalid upgrade after upgrading the Longhorn setup to the new version, so it can ensure the current setup with the old version is intact if the upgrade hook failed. 

If using kubectl apply to do the upgrade, the same behavior will be tackled
```yaml
helmPreUpgradeCheckerJob:
  enabled: false
```

steps.title: Upgrade longhorn to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade longhorn:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade longhorn longhorn/longhorn --namespace longhorn
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n longhorn
helm list -n longhorn
```

steps.title: Confirm [IMPROVEMENT] Remove or Change Helm pre-upgrade… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn -n longhorn --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n longhorn --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: The pre-upgrade hook is only for the helm to prevent any invalid upgrade after upgrading the Longhorn setup to the new version, so it can ensure the current setup with the old version is intact if the upgrade hook failed.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with longhorn installed or the issue environment reproducible.
